title: Generic Types Conversion Library - Configuration Document
description: One YAML/JSON configuration document. A definition with both 'from' and 'to' is concrete (registered as a converter); otherwise it is a base fragment merged beneath concrete definitions.

version.description: Optional configuration schema version (e.g. '1.0'). Absent means the latest supported version.

convert.description: A single conversion definition or a list of them.

definition.description: Concrete when both 'from' and 'to' are present; a base fragment otherwise.

from.description: Fully-qualified source type. Optional; omit (with or instead of 'to') to form a base fragment.

id.description: Identifier of a named base fragment, referenced by 'extends'.

extends.description: Ids of named base fragments to merge beneath this definition, in listed order.

constructor.description: Expression producing the target instance; the no-arg constructor is used when omitted.

fqcn.description: Fully-qualified class/interface name (java.lang types may be unqualified).

policy.description: Action taken when a reference cannot be resolved.

shortcuts.description: Named method/enum-constant references (plain references, not SpEL).

pre.description: Ordered key-val sequence evaluated before construction; each entry publishes exactly one '#variable'.

propertyMap.description: Target property assignments, or (for enum/primitive ranges) source-token -> target mappings. Keys are strings.

propertyValue.description: A single SpEL expression, or a conditional-assignment sequence.

conditionalAssignment.description: Ordered conditions; the first true condition's value is used. Items are single-key maps (condition -> value), optionally ending with a bare fall-through value.
